sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: OpenAI In Talks To Raise Billions From Nvidia, Amazon, Microsoft: Report
feed.summary: OpenAI is reportedly in discussions to secure significant funding, potentially billions, from its key suppliers including NVIDIA, Amazon, and Microsoft to support its expansion plans and substantial data center capacity needs. While reports vary on the exact amount, with some suggesting up to $40 billion and others $60 billion for this round, the company is aiming for $100 billion in total funding with a valuation of $830 billion. This fundraising effort comes as OpenAI also explores testing advertisements in its free tiers and has seen increased worker productivity due to AI advancements, leading to a slower hiring pace.

feed.title: How Magic Lawsuits And New Brand Leaders At Hasbro (HAS) Have Changed Its Investment Story
feed.summary: Hasbro is at a pivotal moment, facing shareholder lawsuits concerning alleged mismanagement and overproduction of its Magic: The Gathering brand, even as it announced a new licensing deal for MONOPOLY Table Games. The company has also added two high-profile consumer and gaming executives to its board, bringing fresh expertise. These developments underscore the scrutiny on Hasbro's brand management and financial health, particularly regarding its key franchises and investment narrative amidst a still-leveraged balance sheet.

feed.title: Coca-Cola eyes protein and fiber enhanced drinks
feed.summary: Coca-Cola is exploring the addition of protein and dietary fiber to its beverages, a move highlighted by CEO James Quincey. While the company sees these as refreshing new avenues, particularly given the success of Diet Coke and Coke Zero, health experts express skepticism, viewing such products as potentially misleading "junk food" due to their limited nutritional benefits compared to overall health guidelines. The initiative is already present in some markets, like Japan with Coca-Cola Fiber+, but critical debate exists around the genuine health value of these enhanced drinks.

feed.title: Arch Insurance International launches event cancellation cyber coverage
feed.summary: Arch Insurance International has introduced new event cancellation cyber coverage, designed to protect events from various disruptions caused by cyber acts or computer system failures. This extension to existing event cancellation policies also covers third-party infrastructure failures and includes access to incident response consultants, forensic investigation, legal counsel, and public relations support. The coverage addresses the increasing demand for cyber protection in events due to rising malicious cyber activity.

feed.title: Accountability Disputes: SoCal Edison sues Pasadena Water and Power and other agencies
feed.summary: Southern California Edison has filed cross-complaints against Pasadena Water and Power, Los Angeles County, Southern California Gas Company, and other agencies, alleging their actions contributed to the Eaton Fire. This move comes over a year after the fire, where SCE's equipment was implicated as a likely cause, with the utility claiming these other agencies failed in timely evacuations and providing sufficient water for firefighting. Pasadena officials are reviewing the cross-complaint, maintaining that Edison's equipment caused the fire and that the utility should accept responsibility.

feed.title: Cisco Expands European Renewable Energy Through Partnership with R.Power S.A. in Poland Facilitated by SR Inc's NZCB
feed.summary: Cisco Systems, Inc. has partnered with R.Power S.A., a leading Polish solar energy developer, through SR Inc's Net Zero Consortium for Buyers (NZCB) to support four new solar projects in Poland. This long-term Virtual Power Purchase Agreement (VPPA) will provide 470 GWh of clean energy and aligns with Cisco's commitment to expanding renewable energy access and strengthening Europe's renewable energy landscape. The partnership will accelerate the use of clean energy in Poland and supports Cisco's operational needs, including its lab facilities in Krakow.

feed.title: PeakShares Sector Rotation ETF (NASDAQ:PSTR) Stock Price Down 0.2% – Here’s What Happened
feed.summary: Shares of PeakShares Sector Rotation ETF (NASDAQ:PSTR) decreased by 0.2% in recent trading, with 1,609 shares exchanging hands, a 48% drop from its average volume. feed.title: Piper Sandler Initiates Coverage on STTK with Overweight Rating
feed.summary: Piper Sandler has initiated coverage on Shattuck Labs (STTK) with an "Overweight" rating and a price target of $15.00 USD, a significant increase from previous analyst targets. This new rating suggests a positive outlook for the biotechnology company, which specializes in developing treatments for inflammatory and immune-mediated diseases. While Wall Street analysts have an average target price of $7.25 for STTK, GuruFocus estimates a one-year GF Value of $0.55, indicating a potential downside.

feed.title: BlackRock Changes Indexes, Fees, For 2 iShares Multifactor ETFs
feed.summary: BlackRock has made changes to two of its iShares Multifactor ETFs, LRGF and INTF, by updating their underlying indexes and adjusting their expense ratios. LRGF will now follow the STOXX US Equity Factor Index with a 0.08% expense ratio, and INTF will track the STOXX International Equity Factor Index with a 0.15% expense ratio. These changes aim to enhance the ETFs' ability to capture diverse factor performance at a low cost, providing investors with a transparent and efficient way to integrate factor-based strategies into their portfolios.

feed.title: Air Products’ New NASA Hydrogen Deals And What They Mean For APD Investors
feed.summary: Air Products and Chemicals (NYSE: APD) has secured new contracts with NASA to supply liquid hydrogen for various space programs, including the Kennedy Space Center. These deals emphasize APD's role in critical space infrastructure and align with its strategy of focusing on large, long-term hydrogen projects. While the contracts are seen as a positive development for recurring cash flows and market positioning against competitors like Linde and Air Liquide, investors are advised to monitor how these translate into actual cash generation given analyst concerns about APD's debt and dividend coverage.

feed.title: Assessing Sempra (SRE) Valuation As Recent Share Price Moves Draw Investor Attention
feed.summary: Sempra's recent share price moves have drawn investor attention, with the stock closing at US$86.78. While a popular narrative suggests the company is 13.1% undervalued with a fair value of $99.87 based on future LNG projects, Simply Wall St's DCF model indicates a fair value of $81.42, suggesting it might be slightly overvalued. The article encourages investors to consider both perspectives and conduct their own research, highlighting available tools for valuation.

The Zacks Consensus Estimate for earnings is 56 cents per share, and for sales, it's $2.15 billion, both indicating year-over-year decreases.

feed.title: Broadcom (AVGO) Strengthens Position in AI Compute and Infrastructure Ecosystem
feed.summary: Broadcom Inc. (AVGO) is highlighted by Goldman Sachs and JPMorgan as a top semiconductor pick due to its strong position in the AI compute and infrastructure ecosystem. Goldman Sachs' research indicates that Broadcom's collaboration with Google on Tensor Processing Units (TPUs) is significantly narrowing the cost efficiency gap with Nvidia's GPU solutions for AI inference. This analysis underscores Broadcom's potential to benefit from continued investment in artificial intelligence infrastructure and advancements in networking technology.

feed.title: Is Analog Devices (ADI) One of Goldman Sachs’ Top Semiconductor Stock Picks?
feed.summary: Analog Devices (ADI) is highlighted as one of Goldman Sachs' top semiconductor stock picks, with TD Cowen and Stifel recently raising their price targets due to its strong positioning in industrial markets, healthy gross profit margins, and resilience. Despite a tempered automotive outlook, analysts expect solid Q1 earnings for fiscal 2026. The article notes ADI's high-performance analog and mixed-signal capabilities, robust B2B revenue, and synergies from the Maxim Integrated acquisition, solidifying its market position.

feed.title: Tesla shares up 1.8% in Frankfurt day after results
feed.summary: Tesla shares experienced a 1.8% increase in Frankfurt following the company's announcement to invest $2 billion in Elon Musk's AI company, xAI. Additionally, Tesla confirmed that production plans for its Cybercab robotaxi are on schedule for the current year. This rise was consistent with aftermarket trading of Tesla's U.S. shares, which had initially seen a larger gain before paring back.

feed.title: Scotiabank Lowers EQT (EQT) PT to $63 While Forecasting Persistent Natural Gas Supply Deficits
feed.summary: Scotiabank has lowered its price target for EQT Corporation (EQT) to $63 from $67, maintaining a Sector Perform rating, while forecasting persistent natural gas supply deficits in North America. This adjustment is part of a bullish outlook on the natural gas sector, with Scotiabank anticipating that fundamental imbalances will drive commodity prices and energy equities higher. Barclays also reduced its price target for EQT to $64, but kept an Overweight rating, noting the resilience of the upstream sector's cash return model despite macroeconomic volatility.
